Implement Data Quality Validation Framework\n434\t\n435\t**Priority:** MEDIUM — Prevents future data quality issues\n436\t\n437\t```python\n438\tclass OptionsDataValidator:\n439\t    \"\"\"Comprehensive validation for options data before processing\"\"\"\n440\t    \n441\t    def validate_row(self, row) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n442\t        \"\"\"\n443\t        Validate a single row of options data.\n444\t        \n445\t        Returns:\n446\t            (is_valid, error_message)\n447\t        \"\"\"\n448\t        checks = [\n449\t            (row['T'] > 0, f\"Invalid T={row['T']} (time to expiration must be > 0)\"),\n450\t            (row['F'] > 0, f\"Invalid F={row['F']} (forward price must be > 0)\"),\n451\t            (row['K'] > 0, f\"Invalid K={row['K']} (strike price must be > 0)\"),\n452\t            (row.get('undiscounted_option_price', 0) > 0, f\"Invalid option price\"),\n453\t        ]\n454\t        \n455\t        for valid, error_msg in checks:\n456\t            if not valid:\n457\t                return False, error_msg\n458\t        return True, \"\"\n459\t    \n460\t    def validate_chunk(self, chunk) -> dict:\n461\t        \"\"\"Validate a chunk of options data and return summary\"\"\"\n462\t        results = {'valid': 0, 'invalid': 0, 'errors': []}\n463\t        \n464\t        for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n465\t            is_valid, error_msg = self.validate_row(row)\n466\t            if is_valid:\n467\t                results['valid'] += 1\n468\t            else:\n469\t                results['invalid'] += 1\n470\t                results['errors'].append({\n471\t                    'symbol': row.get('symbol', 'UNKNOWN'),\n472\t                    'error': error_msg\n473\t                })\n474\t        \n475\t        return results\n476\t```\n477\t\n478\t#### 4. Implement Circuit Breaker Pattern\n511\t\n512\t**Priority:** LOW — Architectural improvement for resilience\n513\t\n514\t```python\n515\timport time\n516\tfrom enum import Enum\n517\t\n518\tclass CircuitState(Enum):\n519\t    CLOSED = 'closed'      # Normal operation\n520\t    OPEN = 'open'          # Failing, reject requests\n521\t    HALF_OPEN = 'half_open'  # Testing if failures resolved\n522\t\n523\tclass OptionsCalculationCircuitBreaker:\n524\t    \"\"\"Prevents cascading failures by stopping calculations when error threshold exceeded\"\"\"\n525\t    \n526\t    def __init__(self, failure_threshold=10, timeout=300):\n527\t        self.failure_threshold = failure_threshold\n528\t        self.timeout = timeout  # seconds to wait before trying again\n529\t        self.failures = 0\n530\t        self.last_failure_time = None\n531\t        self.state = CircuitState.CLOSED\n532\t    \n533\t    def call(self, func, *args, **kwargs):\n534\t        \"\"\"Execute function with circuit breaker protection\"\"\"\n535\t        if self.state == CircuitState.OPEN:\n536\t            if time.time() - self.last_failure_time > self.timeout:\n537\t                self.state = CircuitState.HALF_OPEN\n538\t            else:\n539\t                raise CircuitBreakerOpenError(\"Circuit breaker is OPEN - too many failures\")\n540\t        \n541\t        try:\n542\t            result = func(*args, **kwargs)\n543\t            if self.state == CircuitState.HALF_OPEN:\n544\t                self.state = CircuitState.CLOSED\n545\t                self.failures = 0\n546\t            return result\n547\t        except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n548\t            self.failures += 1\n549\t            self.last_failure_time = time.time()\n550\t            if self.failures >= self.failure_threshold:\n551\t                self.state = CircuitState.OPEN\n552\t            raise\n553\t```\n554\t\n555\t#### 6. Fix ZeroDivisionError in Options Pipeline (CRITICAL)\n\n**Priority:** CRITICAL — **Still actively occurring as of July 24, 2026**  \n**Business Impact:** Eliminates 82+ current errors, prevents 247+ restarts  \n**Timeline:** Implement immediately\n\n**Required Code Solution:**\n```python\ndef calculate_implied_volatility(undiscounted_option_price, F, K, t, flag):\n    \"\"\"\n    Calculate implied volatility with comprehensive input validation.\n    \n    Args:\n        undiscounted_option_price: Option price (must be > 0)\n        F: Forward price (must be > 0)\n        K: Strike price (must be > 0)\n        t: Time to expiration (must be > 0)\n        flag: 'call' or 'put'\n    \n    Returns:\n        Implied volatility or None if inputs are invalid\n    \"\"\"\n    # Comprehensive input validation\n    if t <= 0:\n        logger.warning(f\"Invalid time parameter t={t}, skipping calculation\")\n        return None\n    if F <= 0 or K <= 0:\n        logger.warning(f\"Invalid price parameters F={F}, K={K}, skipping calculation\")\n        return None\n    if undiscounted_option_price <= 0:\n        logger.warning(f\"Invalid option price={undiscounted_option_price}, skipping calculation\")\n        return None\n    \n    # Safe calculation with exception handling\n    try:\n        return vectorized_implied_volatility(\n            undiscounted_option_price, F, K, t, flag\n        )\n    except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Calculation failed: price={undiscounted_option_price}, F={F}, K={K}, t={t}, error={e}\")\n        return None\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Unexpected calculation error: {e}\")\n        return None\n```\n\n**Implementation Steps:**\n1. Add input validation to all calculation entry points\n2. Add comprehensive error handling with logging\n3. Add telemetry for validation failures\n4. Deploy to production with monitoring\n5. Verify ZeroDivisionError elimination in logs\n\n#### 2. Clean Up Failed Pods (Both Systems) (HIGH)\n\n**Priority:** HIGH — Operational hygiene and resource cleanup\n\n```bash\n# Options pipeline cleanup\nkubectl --server=http://traefik-iad-options:8001 delete pod options-greeks-7cbcd5dff4-8db6c -n options --force --grace-period=0\n\n# IBKR MCP cleanup  \nkubectl --server=http://traefik-ardenone-cluster:8001 delete pod ibkr-mcp-server-7d78d47dbb-898mv -n ibkr-mcp --force --grace-period=0\nkubectl --server=http://traefik-ardenone-cluster:8001 delete pod ibkr-mcp-server-7dd7c9c9bc-6cn57 -n ibkr-mcp --force --grace-period=0\n```\n\n### Medium-Term Improvements 🟡\n\n#### 3. Implement Data Quality Validation Framework\n\n**Priority:** MEDIUM — Prevents future data quality issues\n\n```python\nclass OptionsDataValidator:\n    \"\"\"Comprehensive validation for options data before processing\"\"\"\n    \n    def validate_row(self, row) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Validate a single row of options data.\n        \n        Returns:\n            (is_valid, error_message)\n        \"\"\"\n        checks = [\n            (row['T'] > 0, f\"Invalid T={row['T']} (time to expiration must be > 0)\"),\n            (row['F'] > 0, f\"Invalid F={row['F']} (forward price must be > 0)\"),\n            (row['K'] > 0, f\"Invalid K={row['K']} (strike price must be > 0)\"),\n            (row.get('undiscounted_option_price', 0) > 0, f\"Invalid option price\"),\n        ]\n        \n        for valid, error_msg in checks:\n            if not valid:\n                return False, error_msg\n        return True, \"\"\n    \n    def validate_chunk(self, chunk) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Validate a chunk of options data and return summary\"\"\"\n        results = {'valid': 0, 'invalid': 0, 'errors': []}\n        \n        for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n            is_valid, error_msg = self.validate_row(row)\n            if is_valid:\n                results['valid'] += 1\n            else:\n                results['invalid'] += 1\n                results['errors'].append({\n                    'symbol': row.get('symbol', 'UNKNOWN'),\n                    'error': error_msg\n                })\n        \n        return results\n```\n\n#### 4. Add Comprehensive Monitoring and Alerting\n\n**Priority:** MEDIUM — Early detection of future issues\n\n```python\nfrom prometheus_client import Counter, Histogram\n\n# Define metrics\noptions_calculation_failures = Counter(\n    'options_calculation_failures_total',\n    'Total options calculation failures',\n    ['reason']  # zero_division, invalid_input, validation_failed\n)\n\noptions_calculation_success = Counter(\n    'options_calculation_success_total',\n    'Successful options calculations'\n)\n\noptions_processing_duration = Histogram(\n    'options_processing_duration_seconds',\n    'Options data processing duration'\n)\n```\n\n**Alert Thresholds:**\n- **Warning**: >5 calculation failures/hour\n- **Critical**: >10 calculation failures/hour\n- **Emergency**: >50 calculation failures/hour\n\n### Long-Term Architecture 🟢\n\n#### 5. Implement Circuit Breaker Pattern\n\n**Priority:** LOW — Architectural improvement for resilience\n\n```python\nimport time\nfrom enum import Enum\n\nclass CircuitState(Enum):\n    CLOSED = 'closed'      # Normal operation\n    OPEN = 'open'          # Failing, reject requests\n    HALF_OPEN = 'half_open'  # Testing if failures resolved\n\nclass OptionsCalculationCircuitBreaker:\n    \"\"\"Prevents cascading failures by stopping calculations when error threshold exceeded\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, failure_threshold=10, timeout=300):\n        self.failure_threshold = failure_threshold\n        self.timeout = timeout  # seconds to wait before trying again\n        self.failures = 0\n        self.last_failure_time = None\n        self.state = CircuitState.CLOSED\n    \n    def call(self, func, *args, **kwargs):\n        \"\"\"Execute function with circuit breaker protection\"\"\"\n        if self.state == CircuitState.OPEN:\n            if time.time() - self.last_failure_time > self.timeout:\n                self.state = CircuitState.HALF_OPEN\n            else:\n                raise CircuitBreakerOpenError(\"Circuit breaker is OPEN - too many failures\")\n        \n        try:\n            result = func(*args, **kwargs)\n            if self.state == CircuitState.HALF_OPEN:\n                self.state = CircuitState.CLOSED\n                self.failures = 0\n            return result\n        except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n            self.failures += 1\n            self.last_failure_time = time.time()\n            if self.failures >= self.failure_threshold:\n                self.state = CircuitState.OPEN\n            raise\n```\n\n#### 6. ZeroDivisionError Crisis (82 active errors)\n\n**Error Signature:**\n```\n2026-07-24 13:00:47,574 ERROR __main__ - Unexpected error\nZeroDivisionError: division by zero\n```\n\n**Current Activity Analysis (July 24, 2026):**\n- **Time Range**: 13:00:47 to 14:14:57 (1 hour 14 minutes)\n- **Error Count**: 82 distinct ZeroDivisionError instances\n- **Frequency**: Approximately every 45-60 seconds (65 errors/hour)\n- **Status**: ACTIVELY OCCURRING during analysis\n- **Pattern**: Consistent recurring failures with no intervention\n\n**Impact Assessment:**\n- 🔴 **Service Reliability**: Immediate pod termination on each error\n- 🔴 **Data Quality**: Failed calculations produce incomplete options Greeks data\n- 🔴 **Resource Consumption**: Each error triggers pod restart, wasting compute resources\n- 🔴 **Operational Overhead**: Continuous failures require manual intervention\n\n**Root Cause Analysis:**\n```python\n# Missing input validation in calculation code\ndef calculate_greeks(chunk):\n    for row in chunk.iterrows():\n        t = row['T']      # Can be 0 → division by zero\n        F = row['F']      # Can be ≤0 → invalid calculation\n        K = row['K']      # Can be ≤0 → invalid calculation\n        \n        # No validation before calculation → crashes on invalid inputs\n        iv = py_vollib_vectorized.implied_volatility.vectorized_implied_volatility(\n            undiscounted_option_price, F, K, t, flag\n        )\n```\n\n#### 2. Fix ZeroDivisionError in Options Pipeline (CRITICAL)\n\n**Priority:** CRITICAL — **Still actively occurring as of July 24, 2026**  \n**Business Impact:** Eliminates 82+ current errors, prevents service crashes  \n**Timeline:** Implement immediately\n\n**Required Code Solution:**\n```python\ndef calculate_implied_volatility(undiscounted_option_price, F, K, t, flag):\n    \"\"\"\n    Calculate implied volatility with comprehensive input validation.\n    \n    Args:\n        undiscounted_option_price: Option price (must be > 0)\n        F: Forward price (must be > 0)\n        K: Strike price (must be > 0)\n        t: Time to expiration (must be > 0)\n        flag: 'call' or 'put'\n    \n    Returns:\n        Implied volatility or None if inputs are invalid\n    \"\"\"\n    # Comprehensive input validation\n    if t <= 0:\n        logger.warning(f\"Invalid time parameter t={t}, skipping calculation\")\n        return None\n    if F <= 0 or K <= 0:\n        logger.warning(f\"Invalid price parameters F={F}, K={K}, skipping calculation\")\n        return None\n    if undiscounted_option_price <= 0:\n        logger.warning(f\"Invalid option price={undiscounted_option_price}, skipping calculation\")\n        return None\n    \n    # Safe calculation with exception handling\n    try:\n        return vectorized_implied_volatility(\n            undiscounted_option_price, F, K, t, flag\n        )\n    except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Calculation failed: price={undiscounted_option_price}, F={F}, K={K}, t={t}, error={e}\")\n        return None\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Unexpected calculation error: {e}\")\n        return None\n```\n\n**Implementation Steps:**\n1. Add input validation to all calculation entry points\n2. Add comprehensive error handling with logging\n3. Add telemetry for validation failures\n4. Deploy to production with monitoring\n5. Verify ZeroDivisionError elimination in logs\n6. Conduct regression testing to prevent similar issues\n\n### Medium-Term Improvements 🟡\n\n#### 2. Implement Data Quality Validation Framework\n\n**Priority:** MEDIUM — Prevents future data quality issues\n\n**Implementation:**\n```python\nclass OptionsDataValidator:\n    \"\"\"Comprehensive validation for options data before processing\"\"\"\n    \n    def validate_row(self, row) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Validate a single row of options data.\n        \n        Returns:\n            (is_valid, error_message)\n        \"\"\"\n        checks = [\n            (row['T'] > 0, f\"Invalid T={row['T']} (time to expiration must be > 0)\"),\n            (row['F'] > 0, f\"Invalid F={row['F']} (forward price must be > 0)\"),\n            (row['K'] > 0, f\"Invalid K={row['K']} (strike price must be > 0)\"),\n            (row.get('undiscounted_option_price', 0) > 0, f\"Invalid option price\"),\n        ]\n        \n        for valid, error_msg in checks:\n            if not valid:\n                return False, error_msg\n        return True, \"\"\n    \n    def validate_chunk(self, chunk) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Validate a chunk of options data and return summary\"\"\"\n        results = {'valid': 0, 'invalid': 0, 'errors': []}\n        \n        for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n            is_valid, error_msg = self.validate_row(row)\n            if is_valid:\n                results['valid'] += 1\n            else:\n                results['invalid'] += 1\n                results['errors'].append({\n                    'symbol': row.get('symbol', 'UNKNOWN'),\n                    'error': error_msg\n                })\n        \n        return results\n```\n\n#### 3. Improve External Dependency Resilience\n\n**Priority:** MEDIUM — Better service discovery and retry logic\n\n**Implementation:**\n```python\nimport time\nfrom typing import Optional\n\nclass ResilientQueueClient:\n    \"\"\"Queue API client with retry logic and circuit breaker\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, max_retries=3, backoff_factor=2):\n        self.max_retries = max_retries\n        self.backoff_factor = backoff_factor\n    \n    def connect_with_retry(self, url: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Connect to queue API with exponential backoff retry.\n        \n        Returns:\n            True if connection successful, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        for attempt in range(self.max_retries):\n            try:\n                response = requests.get(f\"{url}/health\", timeout=5)\n                if response.status_code == 200:\n                    return True\n            except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:\n                wait_time = self.backoff_factor ** attempt\n                logger.warning(f\"Connection attempt {attempt + 1} failed: {e}. Retrying in {wait_time}s...\")\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\n        \n        logger.error(f\"Failed to connect to {url} after {self.max_retries} attempts\")\n        return False\n```\n\n### Long-Term Excellence Maintenance 🟢\n\n#### 5. Fix ZeroDivisionError in Options Pipeline (CRITICAL)\n\n**Priority:** CRITICAL — **Still actively occurring as of July 24, 2026**  \n**Business Impact:** Eliminates 82+ current errors, prevents service crashes  \n**Timeline:** Implement immediately\n\n**Required Code Solution:**\n```python\ndef calculate_implied_volatility(undiscounted_option_price, F, K, t, flag):\n    \"\"\"\n    Calculate implied volatility with comprehensive input validation.\n    \n    Args:\n        undiscounted_option_price: Option price (must be > 0)\n        F: Forward price (must be > 0)\n        K: Strike price (must be > 0)\n        t: Time to expiration (must be > 0)\n        flag: 'call' or 'put'\n    \n    Returns:\n        Implied volatility or None if inputs are invalid\n    \"\"\"\n    # Comprehensive input validation\n    if t <= 0:\n        logger.warning(f\"Invalid time parameter t={t}, skipping calculation\")\n        return None\n    if F <= 0 or K <= 0:\n        logger.warning(f\"Invalid price parameters F={F}, K={K}, skipping calculation\")\n        return None\n    if undiscounted_option_price <= 0:\n        logger.warning(f\"Invalid option price={undiscounted_option_price}, skipping calculation\")\n        return None\n    \n    # Safe calculation with exception handling\n    try:\n        return vectorized_implied_volatility(\n            undiscounted_option_price, F, K, t, flag\n        )\n    except ZeroDivisionError as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Calculation failed: price={undiscounted_option_price}, F={F}, K={K}, t={t}, error={e}\")\n        return None\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Unexpected calculation error: {e}\")\n        return None\n```\n\n**Implementation Steps:**\n1. Add input validation to all calculation entry points\n2. Add comprehensive error handling with logging\n3. Add telemetry for validation failures\n4. Deploy to production with monitoring\n5. Verify ZeroDivisionError elimination in logs\n6. Conduct regression testing to prevent similar issues\n\n### Medium-Term Improvements 🟡\n\n#### 2. Implement Data Quality Validation Framework\n\n**Priority:** MEDIUM — Prevents future data quality issues\n\n**Implementation:**\n```python\nclass OptionsDataValidator:\n    \"\"\"Comprehensive validation for options data before processing\"\"\"\n    \n    def validate_row(self, row) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Validate a single row of options data.\n        \n        Returns:\n            (is_valid, error_message)\n        \"\"\"\n        checks = [\n            (row['T'] > 0, f\"Invalid T={row['T']} (time to expiration must be > 0)\"),\n            (row['F'] > 0, f\"Invalid F={row['F']} (forward price must be > 0)\"),\n            (row['K'] > 0, f\"Invalid K={row['K']} (strike price must be > 0)\"),\n            (row.get('undiscounted_option_price', 0) > 0, f\"Invalid option price\"),\n        ]\n        \n        for valid, error_msg in checks:\n            if not valid:\n                return False, error_msg\n        return True, \"\"\n    \n    def validate_chunk(self, chunk) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Validate a chunk of options data and return summary\"\"\"\n        results = {'valid': 0, 'invalid': 0, 'errors': []}\n        \n        for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n            is_valid, error_msg = self.validate_row(row)\n            if is_valid:\n                results['valid'] += 1\n            else:\n                results['invalid'] += 1\n                results['errors'].append({\n                    'symbol': row.get('symbol', 'UNKNOWN'),\n                    'error': error_msg\n                })\n        \n        return results\n```\n\n#### 3. Retrying in {wait_time}s...\")\n                time.sleep(wait_time)\n        \n        logger.error(f\"Failed to connect to {url} after {self.max_retries} attempts\")\n        return False\n```\n\n### Long-Term Excellence Maintenance 🟢\n\n#### 5.
